azure-compute: The Azure Compute skill helps you choose, configure, and troubleshoot Azure virtual machines (VMs) and Virtual Machine Scale Sets. It answers questions about VM sizing, pricing, autoscaling, orchestration modes, and connectivity issues but doesn't execute infrastructure changes directly.
azure-deploy: The Azure Deploy skill executes deployments for applications that already have infrastructure definitions and deployment plans. It runs `azd up`, `azd deploy`, Bicep deployments, and Terraform apply commands with built-in error recovery. Use the prepare and validate skills first to ensure your application is ready.
azure-prepare: The Azure Prepare skill helps you set up everything needed to deploy an application to Azure. It generates infrastructure as code (Bicep or Terraform), creates `azure.yaml` configuration, adds Dockerfiles, and configures CI/CD pipelines. This is the first step in the deployment workflow.
azure-validate: The Azure Validate skill runs pre-deployment checks on your application's configuration, infrastructure definitions, RBAC role assignments, managed identity permissions, and prerequisites. It identifies issues before you deploy, saving time and preventing failed deployments.
azure-cloud-migrate: The Azure Cloud Migration skill assesses cross-cloud workloads and generates migration plans with code conversion. It supports migrating AWS Lambda functions to Azure Functions and GCP Cloud Run services to Azure Container Apps, producing readiness reports and converted code.
azure-quotas: The Azure Quotas skill checks and manages Azure resource quotas and usage across providers. It helps with deployment planning, capacity validation, and region selection by querying current limits and usage for compute, networking, and storage resources.
azure-upgrade: The Azure Upgrade skill assesses and upgrades Azure workloads between plans, tiers, or SKUs. It generates assessment reports, checks upgrade readiness, and automates upgrade steps for services like Azure Functions (Consumption to Flex Consumption) and App Service plan changes.
microsoft-foundry: The Microsoft Foundry skill helps you deploy, evaluate, and manage AI agents end-to-end using Azure AI Foundry. It covers model deployment, RAG applications with knowledge indexes, agent creation with tools, batch evaluation, prompt optimization, and continuous monitoring.
